questions.question: What is the main goal of a problem-solving agent?
options: A) To learn from experience
B) To maximize utility or satisfaction
C) To minimize risk or uncertainty
D) To optimize a specific objective function
questions.explanation: A problem-solving agent aims to optimize a specific objective function by searching for the best solution.

questions.question: Which type of problem-solving agent uses a systematic and exhaustive approach to find a solution?
options: A) Heuristic
B) Metaheuristic
C) Greedy
D) Exhaustive
questions.explanation: Exhaustive problem-solving agents use a systematic and exhaustive approach to find a solution by considering all possible options.

questions.question: What is the primary advantage of using a heuristic problem-solving agent?
options: A) It is more efficient than exhaustive agents
B) It guarantees the optimal solution
C) It is more suitable for complex problems
D) It is less expensive to implement
questions.explanation: Heuristic problem-solving agents are more efficient than exhaustive agents as they don't consider all possible options.

questions.question: Which type of problem-solving agent uses a trial-and-error approach to find a solution?
options: A) Heuristic
B) Metaheuristic
C) Greedy
D) Trial-and-Error
questions.explanation: Trial-and-error problem-solving agents use a trial-and-error approach to find a solution by testing options one by one.

questions.question: What is the primary disadvantage of using a trial-and-error problem-solving agent?
options: A) It is more efficient than heuristic agents
B) It guarantees the optimal solution
C) It can get stuck in local optima
D) It is less expensive to implement
questions.explanation: Trial-and-error problem-solving agents can get stuck in local optima, meaning they may not find the global optimum solution.
